_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 5-6 Adult Return of Spontaneous Circulation (Revised 05/30/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Cardiac Protocol Section 5-6: Return of Spontaneous Circulation — MDHHS Approved 5/30/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: This protocol should be followed for all cardiac arrests with ROSC. If arrest is of known traumatic origin, refer to Traumatic Arrest-Treatment Protocol and MCA Transport Protocol. After ROSC, patients should be stabilized on scene for 5–10 minutes prior to transport. Refer to Crashing Adult/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol.

sections.title: Post-ROSC Care
points: Stabilize patient on scene 5–10 minutes before moving — initiate ALS response if available
If ventilation assistance required: ventilate at 10–12 breaths per minute — do NOT hyperventilate
Monitor waveform ETCO2 — if ventilation assistance required, target ETCO2 35–45 mmHg per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol
Monitor vital signs and reassess patient — if patient becomes pulseless, begin CPR and refer to Adult Cardiac Arrest General-Treatment Protocol
Check blood glucose (may be MFR skill — see Blood Glucose Testing-Procedure Protocol)
Start IV/IO NS or LR KVO if not already in place
Treat hypotension (SBP < 90 mmHg) with IV/IO fluid bolus up to 1 liter
Perform 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection: BLS or Specialist — see 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol)
Consider transport to facility capable of Percutaneous Coronary Intervention (PCI) per MCA protocol if 12-lead ECG indicates STEMI
If hypotension persists after initial IV/IO fluid bolus: prepare push-dose epinephrine while administering second 1-liter fluid bolus (maximum total fluid 2 liters)

Administer push-dose epinephrine for persistent hypotension: prepare 10 mcg/mL by adding 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL in 9 mL NS; administer 10–20 mcg (1–2 mL) IV/IO; repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate SBP > 90 mmHg
Anticipate airway intolerance and prepare for patient sedation — if patient becomes agitated with advanced airway in place, refer to Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
If mechanical ventilator available or spontaneous respirations in non-intubated patient: titrate inspired oxygen to maintain SpO2 ≥ 92% but < 98%; titrate ETCO2 between 35–45 mmHg
Consider removal of airway device ONLY if patient is wide awake, following commands, and unable to tolerate the device

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may designate blood glucose testing as an MFR skill
MCA must select provider level authorized to obtain 12-lead ECG post-ROSC

key_drugs.name: Push-Dose Epinephrine IV/IO (Post-ROSC Hypotension)
key_drugs.dose: 10–20 mcg (1–2 mL of 10 mcg/mL solution) IV/IO — repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate SBP > 90 mmHg
key_drugs.indication: Hypotension (SBP < 90 mmHg) persisting after initial IV/IO fluid bolus
key_drugs.contraindications: Use caution in suspected cardiogenic shock
key_drugs.notes: Preparation: add 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL (1:10,000) to 9 mL NS to make 10 mcg/mL concentration. If hypotension persists after the initial bolus, prepare push-dose epinephrine while administering the second 1 L bolus; maximum total fluid is 2 L.

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO Fluid Bolus (Post-ROSC Hypotension)
key_drugs.dose: Up to 1 liter IV/IO — repeat once for total maximum 2 liters if hypotension persists
key_drugs.contraindications: Signs of pulmonary edema — monitor after each bolus
key_drugs.notes: Assess between boluses. Maximum total 2 liters. If hypotension persists after the initial bolus, prepare push-dose epinephrine while administering the second 1 L bolus.

deterioration_flags: Patient becomes pulseless — begin CPR immediately; return to Adult Cardiac Arrest General protocol
SBP < 90 mmHg — IV/IO fluid bolus up to 1L; if persists after first bolus, initiate push-dose epinephrine while giving second bolus (max 2L total)
STEMI on post-ROSC 12-lead — notify hospital; consider PCI-capable facility transport per MCA protocol
Agitation with advanced airway — Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
SpO2 > 98% — titrate oxygen down; hyperoxia may be harmful post-ROSC
ETCO2 < 35 mmHg — reduce ventilation rate; avoid hyperventilation
ETCO2 > 45 mmHg — increase ventilation rate slightly; reassess airway
scope_notes: Monitoring vitals, SpO2, ETCO2, ventilation rate management: all levels
Blood glucose testing: all levels — may be MFR skill per MCA selection
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
Advanced airway sedation: Specialist skill per Patient Procedural Sedation-Procedure Protocol
SpO2 target post-ROSC: ≥ 92% but < 98%; ETCO2 target: 35–45 mmHg
